_doc: Map lenses. Each preset = a named layer set + initial fit_bounds, mirroring the four Folium HTMLs in docs/maps/. Choosing a preset replaces the active layer set; opening a wiki page after that ADDS the page's needed layers without unloading the preset.

tributary_explorer.label: Tributary explorer
tributary_explorer.subtitle: Rivers + downstream + built / under-construction hydro
tributary_explorer.description: The general-purpose Nepal river view: tributaries, Nepal-origin downstream systems extending beyond the border, and the 261 hydropower projects that are operating or licensed to build. The 311 survey-only sites stay one toggle away in the layer panel. Mirrors docs/maps/nepal_tributary_explorer.html.

tributary_network.label: River network
tributary_network.subtitle: Rivers + downstream, no project clutter
tributary_network.description: Same hydrology as the explorer but without the 572-project cloud. Use when you want to read the river geometry on its own. Mirrors docs/maps/nepal_tributary_network.html.

geopolitics.label: Geopolitics
geopolitics.subtitle: Where Nepal sits in Gangetic basin geography
geopolitics.description: Basin polygons (Nepal-linked + India comparison), origin/control callouts, downstream impact markers, and India reference rivers turned on by default. Answers: which Gangetic systems route through Nepal, which don't, and how much of each upstream basin lies inside the border. Mirrors docs/maps/nepal_geopolitics_river_influence.html.

power_system.label: Power system
power_system.subtitle: Grid, gateways, priority projects, storage
power_system.description: Traced transmission corridors, grid hubs, cross-border interconnections, the curated priority watchlist, the storage shortlist, and the full hydropower cloud. River layers stay available but start off so the network reads as a power system rather than another hydro trace. Mirrors docs/maps/nepal_power_system_explorer.html.

wiki.label: Wiki minimal
wiki.subtitle: Sparse base · let the page drive the layers
wiki.description: A near-empty starting canvas. Useful when you want the wiki page (and its binding's layers_on) to entirely drive what shows up on the map.
